steps.title: Identify the zeros
steps.content: The graph passes through $(-6,0)$, $(2,0)$, and $(5,0)$. Points with y-coordinate $0$ are zeros of the polynomial, so the zeros are $-6$, $2$, and $5$.

steps.title: Apply the factor theorem
steps.content: By the Factor Theorem, if $f(a)=0$, then $x-a$ is a factor. The zero $2$ gives the factor $x-2$.

steps.title: Match the listed factor
steps.content: The other zeros would give factors $x+6$ and $x-5$, which are not listed. The listed factor that must divide the polynomial is $x-2$. The answer is C.
